title: Nursery Rhyme. CLXII. Songs.
content:
[The following catch is found in Ben Jonson's 'Masque of Oberon,' and is a most common nursery song at the present day.]

        Buz, quoth the blue fly,
            Hum, quoth the bee,
        Buz and hum they cry,
            And so do we:
        In his ear, in his nose,
            Thus, do you see?
        He ate the dormouse,
            Else it was he.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CLXII. Songs.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
